characters.name: Alex Katsopolis
characters.description: CharacterAlex KatsopolisBasic infoFull nameAlexander Donaldson-KatsopolisNicknameAlex (by everyone)GenderMaleBornNovember 12, 1991Age30StatusAliveEthnicityCaucasianSocial infoResidenceLos Angeles, CaliforniaOccupationFish Taco VendorFamilyJesse Katsopolis(father)Rebecca Donaldson-Katsopolis(mother)Nicky Katsopolis(twin brother)Pamela Katsopolis(adoptive younger sister)Pamela Tanner† (paternal aunt; deceased)Danny Tanner(paternal uncle-in-law)D.J. Tanner-Fuller(paternal cousin)Stephanie Tanner(paternal cousin)Michelle Tanner(paternal cousin)Irene Katsopolis (paternal grandmother)Nick Katsopolis(paternal grandfather)Nedra Donaldson (maternal grandmother)Kenneth Donaldson (maternal grandfather)Corky Donaldson (maternal uncle)Connie Donaldson (maternal aunt)Papouli Katsopolis† (great-grandfather; deceased)Gina Katsopolis (paternal great-grandmother)Jackson Fuller(paternal first cousin, once removed)Max Fuller(paternal first cousin, once removed)Tommy Fuller Jr.(paternal first cousin, once removed)Tommy Fuller† (paternal cousin-in-law; deceased)Danielle Tanner-Gibbler(paternal first cousin, once removed)Tiffany Tanner-Gibbler(paternal first cousin, once removed)Jimmy Gibbler(paternal cousin-in-law)Steve Hale(paternal cousin-in-law)Ramona Gibbler(god first cousin, once removed)Production infoFirst episodeOur Very First Show, AgainLast episodeFuller ThanksgivingEpisode count2Seasons1-2-3-4-5Portrayed byDylan Tuomy-WilhoitAlexander "Alex" Donaldson-Katsopolis(born November 12, 1991) is aspecial guest characteronFuller House.He is a fictional character onFull HouseandFuller House. He is the son ofJesseandBeckyand identical twin brother ofNicky. He is portrayed byDylan Tuomy-Wilhoit. He grew up in the house with the Tanner family. Becky got pregnant with Alex and his twin brother Nicky and had trouble breaking the news to Jesse. She tries to tell him in a candlelight dinner, but Jesse was too happy to go on tour. Later she told him in a game of Pictionary. On Michelle's birthday they had the twins. In "Nicky and/or Alexander", Jesse mixes them up by taking off their booties and put Alex's booties on Nicky. Later he inks Alex's foot. Then Becky finds out. Alex, much like Nicky, is a humorous and mischievous character. Throughout the seventh and eighth seasons, they caused trouble worth more than the two of them combined, let alone singular. They adore each other, which caused them to cling on each other until Jesse and Becky had them find their own personalities. They also were the focus of Jesse's attention, and he tried to raise them the way that his own father never had, but it usually led to them overpowering him until Becky comes in with her motherly advice to him. Nevertheless, they are both still mischievous and cause much stress to their parents' lives. -Thanks to the Full House Wiki (Our Sisters Wiki)

characters.name: Bobby Popko
characters.description: Ramona Gibbler(ex-girlfriend)Lola Wong  Robert Bobby Popkois aguest characterfromFuller House.He'sJackson's best friend, or as he likes to put it, his "Kimmy Gibbler". Bobby likes to live life on the extreme side, urging his friends to do dangerous and extreme activities including skateboarding off roofs and wrestling while he records and ultimately uploads the videos to YouTube. According toMax, Bobby's parents are very rarely at home, leaving Bobby by himself most of the time. He was alsoRamona's first kiss and first boyfriend, however, they split up inseason 3.

characters.name: Buttercup
characters.description: CharacterButtercupNormalUnicornBasic infoFull nameButtercupNicknameUni[1]GenderFemale (supposedly)BornUnknownAgeUnknownStatusAliveEthnicityUnknownSocial infoResidenceEitherThe Gibbler HouseorThe Fuller-Gibbler HomeOccupationHorseFriendsRamona Gibbler(owner)Kimmy Gibbler(owner)Production infoFirst episode"M-m-m-My Ramona"Last episode"Happily Ever After" Buttercup, briefly known as Uni and formerly known as Dave[2], is a recurring character/pet onFuller House. She is portrayed by an unknown horse. She was a gift fromKimmy Gibblerto her daughterRamona Gibbler, and is sometimes referred to as 'the dancing horse.' Her latest appearance was inHappily Ever After, when she went to Ramona's homecoming dance dressed like a unicorn. Nothing much of a relationship is shown between these two characters except how Ramona cares about Buttercup and how Ramona is astounded by how Buttercup can dance.

characters.name: Chief Mulrooney
characters.description: CharacterChief MulrooneyBasic infoGenderMaleStatusAliveEthnicityCaucausionSocial infoResidenceSan Francisco, CaliforniaProduction infoFirst episodeSecrets, Lies, & FiretrucksLast episodeSecrets, Lies, & FiretrucksEpisode count1Portrayed byPatrick Gallagher Chief Mulrooneyis an guest character inFuller House.He is the new chief at the Fire Station thatTommy Fuller Sr.used to work at. On July 13, of 1991, Wake up San Francisco was doing a segment on public servants with interesting hobbies, which he was accepted to go on as "The Singing Fireman". However, sinceDanny Tannerallowed "the breakdancing paramedic" do to two numbers, the show ran out of time and couldn't feature him. He had never forgiven Danny for this, and still held a grudge as shown inSecrets, Lies, & Firetrucks He is portrayed byPatrick Gallagher.

Cosmo is the son ofComet Jr. Jr.and the great-grandson of Comet, the Tanner family's former pet. The dog who played Cosmo passed away during surgery on December 16th, 2019.

characters.name: Crystal
characters.description: CharacterCrystalBasic infoGenderFemaleStatusAliveSocial infoOccupationFitness SpecialistFamilyMike(grandfather)FriendsMatt Harmon(ex)Production infoFirst episodeWelcome BackPortrayed byGianna DiDonato Crystalis aminor characterinFuller House.Crystal isMatt's former girlfriend. It is possible he is with her to makeD.J.jealous. She is portrayed byGianna DiDonato. In theFuller Houseseason 2official trailer, for a brief moment, it shows a clip showing both Matt andStevetelling D.J. that they both got new girlfriends (Steve's new girlfriend isC.J.), right before D.J. was about to announce who she finally chose. Once that is said,Kimmywhispers toStephanie, "Geez, this poor girls life is aLifeTimemovie waiting to happen!"

characters.name: Cteve
characters.description: RelationshipCteveCharacters shippedSteve HaleC.J.StatusBroken upRivalsDtevePortrayed byScott WeignerVirginia WilliamsCteveis a pairing betweenSteveandC.J.inFuller House.They are currently exes. In "Welcome Back", right whenD.J.was about to choose between Steve andMatt(later revealed that she was going to pick Steve) both Steve and Matt revealed they had girlfriends (with Steve's being C.J.), making D.J. jealous. It was a running gag inseason 2how similar C.J. and D.J. were. In "D.J. and Kimmy's High School Reunion", Steve tells D.J. that he is going to propose to C.J., and she seems a little surprised by this information, but is happy for Steve. In "Happy New Year, Baby", Steve gets extremely nervous and doesn't think he can propose. D.J. then allows him to test it out on her. C.J. walks in the room when Steve is pretending to propose to D.J. and C.J. leaves believing that he was cheating on her. Steve then chases after her, but (still nervous) can't get the words out of his mouth. Steve then tosses D.J. the ring and allows her to propose to C.J. for him. C.J. gets happy knowing that Steve was going to propose to her, and says yes.

characters.name: D.J. Tanner-Hale
characters.description: CharacterDJ Tanner-Fuller-HaleBasic infoFull nameDonna-Jo Margaret Tanner-Fuller-HaleNicknameDJDeejDr. FullerSinead O'ConnerShe-WolfDoombuggyReady Player FullerGenderFemaleBornFebruary 9, 1977Age46StatusAliveEthnicityCaucasian/WhiteSocial infoResidenceSan Francisco, CaliforniaThe Fuller HomeOccupationVeterinarianFamilyDanny Tanner(Father)Pamela Tanner† (Mother Deceased)Teri Tanner(Former Stepmother)Stephanie Tanner(Younger Sister)Michelle Tanner(Younger Sister)Tommy Fuller† (Deceased First Husband)Steve Hale(Second Husband)Jackson Fuller(Son)Max Fuller(Son)Tommy Fuller Jr(Son)Jimmy Gibbler(Brother-in-law)Danielle Tanner-Gibbler(Niece)Tiffany Tanner-Gibbler(Niece)Jesse Katsopolis(Maternal Uncle)Rebecca Katsopolis(Maternal Aunt-in-Law)Nicky Katsopolis(Maternal Cousin)Alex Katsopolis(Maternal Cousin)Pamela Katsopolis(Adoptive Maternal Cousin)Nick Katsopolis(Maternal Grandfather)Irene Katsopolis(Maternal Grandmother)Papouli Katsopolis† (Maternal Great-Grandfather)Gina Katsopolis (Maternal Great-Grandmother)Stavros (Cousin)Claire Tanner(Paternal Grandmother)Wendy Tanner (Paternal Aunt)Steve Tanner (Cousin)Ramona Gibbler(Goddaughter)FriendsKimmy Gibbler(Best Friend)Kathy SantoniNelson BurkhardViperJimmy GibblerRamona Gibbler(Goddaughter)Joey GladstoneFernando Hernandez Guerero Fernandez GuerreroGia MahanEnemiesFernando Hernandez Guerero Fernandez Guerrero(formerly)Gia Mahan(formerly)Production infoFirst episodeOur Very First Show, AgainLast episodeOur Very Last Show, AgainEpisode count75Seasons1•2•3•4•5Portrayed byCandace Cameron Bure  Donna-Jo Margaret "DJ" Tanner-Fuller-Hale(néeTanner)  (born December 1977), is the oldestTannersister onFull House.She is now themain protagoniston theNetflixoriginal show,Fuller House.She is the older sister ofStephanieandMichelle Tanner, and the mother ofJackson,Max, andTommy Jr. Fuller(named after his father). Her husband,Tommy Fuller, died before the events ofseason 1, making D.J. a widow. Since then, Stephanie and D.J.'s best friend,Kimmyhave moved in to help D.J. get back on her feet. In season 1, D.J. reunites withSteve Hale, her old high school boyfriend, who still loves her. D.J. kindly rejects him as she doesn't feel ready to be in a relationship yet. She eventually meets her new co-workerMatt Harmon, who ends up becoming her boyfriend. This causes a love triangle that makes up most of D.J.'s season 1 storyline. In the season finale, D.J. decides to choose neither Steve nor Matt. She often feels guilty when about to break bad news to her kids, and usually bribes them to get them in a better mood before she breaks the news. This is first seen in the second episode of the series, "Moving Day", when she has to tell Jackson that Kimmy and her daughter,Ramonaare moving in with them (in which she makes him a special breakfast of pancakes and milkshakes). This is also shown in the first episode ofseason 3("Best Summer Ever"), where she has to tell Jackson that he is going to Summer School (in which she packs him an iT
characters.personality_section: She often feels guilty when about to break bad news to her kids, and usually bribes them to get them in a better mood before she breaks the news. This is first seen in the second episode of the series, "Moving Day", when she has to tell Jackson that Kimmy and her daughter,Ramonaare moving in with them (in which she makes him a special breakfast of pancakes and milkshakes). This is also shown in the first episode ofseason 3("Best Summer Ever"), where she has to tell Jackson that he is going to Summer School (in which she packs him an iTunes gift card with his lunch). In the season 1 episode, "The Legend of El Explosivo", D.J. downloads an app called "Mothers Intuition" to track Jackson, making sure he is not hanging out withBobby Popko(who she thinks is a bad influence). This episode shows how D.J. can be a little over-protective when it comes to her kids. She is similar to her father Danny Tanner. She also seems to over-do things sometimes, like in theseason 3episode, "Break a Leg" where D.J. decides to be Stephanie's life coach, but ends up accidentally breaking Stephanie's leg in the process. Ever since D.J. was young, she wanted to be a veterinarian, and it is shown inFuller House, that D.J. now works as a veterinarian atHarmon-Fuller Pet Care. And she is thrilled when in the season 3 episode, "M-m-m-My Ramona" Jackson gains interest in the idea of being a veterinarian like her. She is quite similar to Danny Tanner in the originalFull Houseseries, as she is a single, widow

Ever since D.J. was young, she wanted to be a veterinarian, and it is shown inFuller House, that D.J. now works as a veterinarian atHarmon-Fuller Pet Care. She is quite similar to Danny Tanner in the originalFull Houseseries, as she is a single, widow

characters.name: Danny Tanner
characters.description: CharacterDanny TannerBasic infoFull nameDaniel Ernest TannerNicknameDannyGenderMaleBornDecember 11 1957StatusAliveEthnicityCaucasianSocial infoResidence1882 Gerard StreetSan Francisco, CaliforniaOccupationFormer TV News HostFamilyClaire Tanner(mother)Wendy Tanner(sister)Pamela Tanner†  (wife; deceased)Teri Tanner(ex-wife)D.J. Tanner-Fuller(daughter)Stephanie Tanner(daughter)Michelle Tanner(daughter)Jesse Katsopolis(brother-in-law)Rebecca Donaldson-Katsopolis(co-sister-in-law)Nicky Katsopolis(nephew)Alex Katsopolis(nephew)Pamela Katsopolis(adoptive goddaughter)Steve Tanner (nephew)Nick Katsopolis(father-in-law)Irene Katsopolis(mother-in-law)Papouli Katsopolis† (grandfather-in-law; deceased)Gina Katsopolis (grandmother-in-law)Stavros (cousin-in-law)Tommy Fuller†  (son-in-law; deceased)Steve Hale(son-in-law)Jackson Fuller(grandson)Max Fuller(grandson)Tommy Fuller Jr.(grandson)Jimmy Gibbler(son-in-law)Danielle Tanner-Gibbler(granddaughter)Tiffany Tanner-Gibbler(granddaughter)Ramona Gibbler(god granddaughter)FriendsJesse KatsopolisJoey Gladstone(best friend)Vicky Larson(ex-fiancée)Rebecca DonaldsonEnemiesJoey Gladstone(best friend) CurrentlyProduction infoFirst episodeOur Very First Show, AgainLast episodeOur Very Last Show, AgainEpisode count15Seasons1•2•3•4•5Portrayed byBob SagetDaniel Ernest "Danny" Tanneris the main character ofFull Houseand aspecial guest characteronFuller House.Danny has three wonderful daughters,D.J.,Stephanie, andMichelle. His wife,Pamela(Pam), wasJesse's sister. Danny likes a clean house and is said to give some of the best hugs around. San Francisco, California He was portrayed by the lateBob Saget. Danny is the archetype of the "not-hip dad" or the "uncool" character. He sees himself as the "raddest, baddest" dad ever (which his daughters do not agree with him about at all). In episodes like "Ol' Brown Eyes" in season four or "To Joey, With Love" in season eight, he wants to show the others that he has the same coolness that Jesse has. In "Ol Brown Eyes", when Danny is dressed like a rock star, D.J. finds him to be very embarrassing. Danny is also obsessed with cleaning, and wants to make sure there's no speck of dust, dirt, or mildew on anything he owns. His motto is, "Clean is good, dirt is bad." D.J. and Stephanie do not like cleaning as much as Danny thinks they do. Once, Stephanie had confessed that one Christmas, when she asked Santa for a cleaning tool, she did not know he would actually bring it to her. In the episode "Honey, I Broke the House", he even seems to care more about the mess that Stephanie made more than the fact that she drove Joey's new car through the kitchen wall. However, in the episode "The Trouble with Danny" in season five, he decided to find a balance between clean and dirty. In the episode "Joey Goes Hollywood", he revealed that his favorite scent is Lemon Pledge. He got Michelle to become his special helper on his "Christmas" (Spring cleaning), making her hope that he would be proud of her if
characters.personality_section: Danny is the archetype of the "not-hip dad" or the "uncool" character. He sees himself as the "raddest, baddest" dad ever (which his daughters do not agree with him about at all). In episodes like "Ol' Brown Eyes" in season four or "To Joey, With Love" in season eight, he wants to show the others that he has the same coolness that Jesse has. In "Ol Brown Eyes", when Danny is dressed like a rock star, D.J. finds him to be very embarrassing. Danny is also obsessed with cleaning, and wants to make sure there's no speck of dust, dirt, or mildew on anything he owns. His motto is, "Clean is good, dirt is bad." D.J. and Stephanie do not like cleaning as much as Danny thinks they do. Once, Stephanie had confessed that one Christmas, when she asked Santa for a cleaning tool, she did not know he would actually bring it to her. In the episode "Honey, I Broke the House", he even seems to care more about the mess that Stephanie made more than the fact that she drove Joey's new car through the kitchen wall. However, in the episode "The Trouble with Danny" in season five, he decided to find a balance between clean and dirty. In the episode "Joey Goes Hollywood", he revealed that his favorite scent is Lemon Pledge. He got Michelle to become his special helper on his "Christmas" (Spring cleaning), making her hope that he would be proud of her if she was very clean and tidy. He temporarily abandons his clean persona in the episode "Fuller Thanksgiving" after a bookshelf almost fell on him whil

characters.name: Datt
characters.description: RelationshipDattCharacters shippedMatt HarmonD.J Tanner-FullerStatusBroken up, still friends/business partnersRivalsDtevePortrayed byJohn BrothertonCandace Cameron Bure Dattis a romantic/friendship pairing betweenD.J. FullerandMatt HarmononFuller House. Datt is one of the two most popularFuller Houseships strongly rivalingDteve(D.J. and Steve). The ship first started in the episode, "Secrets, Lies, and Firetrucks", where D.J. hangs out withher boss' son, Matt. On D.J.'s way out, Matt yells "D.J., wait!" and she immediately runs up to him and kisses him. After their kiss Matt says that he was just going to tell her that she forgot her purse. Embarrassed, D.J. leaves. Once D.J. leaves, Matt does a short victory dance as he is heading to clean their table.

characters.name: Duane Moffat
characters.description: CharacterDuane MoffatBasic infoFull nameDuane MoffatGenderMaleStatusAliveProduction infoFirst episodeFull HouseLast episodeDJ and Kimmy's High School ReunionEpisode count1Portrayed byScott Menville Duaneis the boyfriend ofKimmy Gibblerin the eight season ofFull Housenoted for his inability to say anything other than "Whatever" (his recurring phrase) to anyone, including Kimmy, though in the finale episode "Michelle Rides Again, Part 2" he is able to quote Shakespeare, which he is passionate about. Kimmy and Duane almost elope in Vegas out of fear that she will loseD.J.if they go to different colleges. Duane makes a reappearance in the episode "DJ and Kimmy's High School Reunion", where he is shown to be more talkative and has turned his "whatever" catchphrase into his mantra as a motivational speaker. He helps to motivate Kimmy (who is down because the reunion has been a disaster for her) to embrace the jokes and enjoy her prom. After she kisses him on the cheek in gratitude, he reverts back to his old personality and is unable to say anything other than "whatever".

characters.name: Gene
characters.description: CharacterGeneBiographical InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationProfessionStudentAppearancesOnly"My Little Hickey"Count1Portrayed byRoni Akurati Geneis a minor character onFuller House. He is friends withJackson,MankowskiandNugs, and likely attends the same school as them. According to Jackson, Gene has seen every movie ever made, and most of his lines in "My Little Hickey" (his only appearance) are quotes from movies. He seems to never make eye contact with people, from his conversation withD.J. Tanner-Fuller. He also says in the episode, that he doesn't like it when other people quote movies.

characters.name: Gia Mahan
characters.description: CharacterGia Mahan-HarmonBasic infoFull nameGianna Mahan-HarmonNicknameGiaGenderFemaleBornDecember 19, 1980Age40 (as of Fuller House season 5)StatusAliveEthnicityCaucasian/WhiteSocial infoResidenceSan Francisco, CaliforniaFamilyUnnamed FatherClaire Mahan (Mother)Roxanne Mahan(Daughter)Unknown Other KidsThree Unnamed Ex-husbandsMatt Harmon(Husband)FriendsStephanie TannerMickeyKimmy GibblerMatt Harmon(husband)DJ Tanner-FullerEnemiesStephanie Tanner(Formerly),D.J. Tanner-Fuller(at times/Formerly)Production infoFirst episodeGirl TalkLast episodeOur Very Last Show, AgainEpisode count11Seasons2•3•4•5Portrayed byMarla SokoloffGianna "Gia" Mahanis aspecial guest characteronFuller House.Gia isStephanie's best friend in Seasons 7 and 8 (8 episodes) onFull House. She is portrayed byMarla Sokoloff. Gia returned forSeason 2in the episode,Girl Talk. Gia is now a soccer mom and will mostly wear black. Her return will also feature the return ofGirl Talk, the band her and Stephanie started along withKimmyand Melissa Morgan. Gia also walks for nine different diseases and though she no longer smokes cigarettes, she is shown to smoke banana-bread flavored electronic cigarettes. She returns again inSeason 3as it is revealed her daughterRoxanneis a classmate ofJackson's in summer school who draws a cat face on him and a tulip in the small of his back with permanent marker when he falls asleep in class. She makes two later appearances being called by Stephanie when D.J. blames Rocki for Jackson's newfound rebellious behavior and later at Ramona's homecoming dance party to perform "I'm a Believer" by The Monkees with her old band Girl Talk. Gia might be considered Stephanie’s Kimmy. Just asD.J.let Kimmy lead her astray a little at times, Stephanie got involved in a small number of wild, irresponsible things because of Gia, such as joyriding and attending Gia's make-out party when Gia's mother was not home.

characters.name: Guest Characters
characters.description: This article is a list of Guest Characters inFuller House.

characters.name: Harry Takayama
characters.description: CharacterHarry TakayamaBasic infoFull nameHarry TakayamaGenderMaleStatusAliveEthnicityAsian-AmericanSocial infoOccupationCertified Public AccountantFamilyUnnamed WifeFriendsStephanie TannerProduction infoFirst episode"Love is in the Air"Last episode"Our Very Last Show, AgainEpisode count2Seasons1,5Portrayed byNathan Nishiguchi (Full House)Michael Sun-Lee (Fuller House)Harry Takayamais a guest character on both Full and Fuller House. He was first introduced in the Full House season 2 episodeD.J.'s Very First Horseand made his last Full House appearance inNerd for a Day, but was last mentioned inOur Very First Telethon. He made his first Fuller House appearance inWar of the Rosesand his last one inOur Very Last Show, Again Michael Sun-Lee (Fuller House) In Full House, Harry is described as a nice, easy-going kid, however seems to be influenced a lot by the people around him. He does not seem to be fond of mathematics, and mentions that he's ready to drop out of school and become a Boy Scout about his frustration with subtraction. He is a close friend of Stephanie and was always welcome to the Tanner House. He and Stephanie got 'married' in the Full House episode 'Middle Age Crazy'.

characters.name: Hunter Pence
characters.description: Cast & CrewHunter PenceFull nameHunter Andrew PenceBirthplaceFort Worth, TexasBirthdateApril 13, 1983OccupationMajor League baseball right fielderNationalityAmericanRoleHimself Hunter Penceis a Major League Baseball right fielder for the Texas Rangers. He previously played for the Houston Astros, Philadelphia Phillies, and San Francisco Giants. Pence stands 6 feet 4 inches tall and weighs 220 pounds. He makes a guest appearance in theFuller HouseepisodeA Giant Leap. Pence made his major league debut as the Houston Astros center fielder on April 28, 2007, versus the Milwaukee Brewers and got his first major league hit and scored his first run. Pence's first home run in the majors was a grand slam, against the St. Louis Cardinals on May 5. Pence hit a dramatic walk-off home run against José Mesa of the Philadelphia Phillies in the bottom of the 13th inning at Minute Maid Park on July 3 in a 5–4 win. It was Mesa's only pitch of the game. Pence was named National League co-Player of the Week for May 14–20, after recording a 1.091 slugging percentage and .625 on-base percentage with 2 home runs and 5 RBIs.
